textByHash.026f1dd904643b8c13f35cc8b0bdb68a1dab9527: Didn't you already ask me questions about this? You were investigating the case and got all that evidence. Besides, Anna was proven guilty - by one of her own siblings.
textByHash.079fcebfd39c781a4ff6f209999e7d4d692f7c70: I was told if I left the house that I would be killed!
textByHash.0a1b20be457538b5d3c21225e0e7281034b1cdc8: Well, I do know that Elizabeth was extremely annoyed by the mosquito nest under the fountain in the garden, and was going to do something about it. I suspect any poison she bought would have been
textByHash.18959360704439440518ef5628185039ce76b4f6: Thank you for all your help in solving the murder.
textByHash.1ad1f02c30412ba161e35d1240cebd085c13f98c: enough to get rid of it. A Good job too, I hate mosquitos.
textByHash.3c0af96c1240c2263fb84fe859a689f43107a5bb: No, I only saw Lord Sinclair handle it. It was on display in the library.
textByHash.404efd403f6fae0f4869f1462334dbe3c4f9a059: I was assisting the cook with the evening meal. I gave Mary His Lordships' dinner, and sent her to take it to him, then heard the scream as she found the body.
textByHash.479e1f480ffd22db6fe8c629d634f3144f760807: Anna? She was a spoiled brat, that's for sure.
textByHash.489deda9135c59523dddba88037e3134e85941a9: No, I only saw Lord Sinclair handle it.
textByHash.4b8da1de68e54746485f413ce289e3a77fe26a89: No, I definitely didn't hear anything like that.
textByHash.5d41326ba46011b6b1cae97534307e038f8019fb: I'm not sure; it could be Anna's or David's.
textByHash.5f99082d099f6769cbb46a9b73eb6bd17e469a26: room about the way he treats the staff, the other day. I did not intend to overhear their conversation, but they were shouting so loudly I could not help but Overhear it. David definitely used the words
textByHash.65d6e9af93a69a22fc9aa17b9a7880fe8bddcb5e: Well, now that you have me thinking about it, I believe I saw Frank handle it once. I'm not really sure what he was doing with it, but I do remember him looking at it.
textByHash.7e4759c78029a766f9a259309f31213eb5880b1f: Well, now that you have me thinking about it, I believe I saw David handle it once. I'm not really sure what he was doing with it, but I do remember him looking at it.
textByHash.8fb224bd4fe334f0bc8cf392c4fa67b7272d47f0: 'I am going to kill you!' as well. I think he should be the prime suspect. He has a nasty temper that one.
textByHash.943a1da13142252c221d02d9435ed1203b6cc124: This is private property! Please leave!
textByHash.969fd13c2e23aed075d1949d5d10a82ec928b8cb: You'd really have to ask her though.
textByHash.9fc9bc32991a8ffa76108e698a1429d488fd0b93: I personally did not; I do not know about the others.
textByHash.a201cca2267cd051912cfa4d34640d84fd68b01b: I was tidying the bedrooms most of the afternoon and then assisted Louisa in the kitchen, preparing dinner. I gave Lord Sinclair's dinner to Mary, and shortly there after heard her screaming.
textByHash.b33ac1c6b0e46195a011f43b2dcd5abea21c7426: I'm under strict orders not to speak to anyone not of the Sinclair family.
textByHash.d6354cc4c7b964b19a1ea457031aeff21d4f7778: I don't know anything about that.
textByHash.dc3899b5ab6670fcdbd119d474fe84c4542bb962: I'm afraid I didn't see her. I didn't meet anyone when I walked from the bedrooms to the kitchen.
textByHash.e7fcb9a1500077ceb8bde2d2981ec288ae12648c: Well, in my considered opinion it must be David. The man is nothing more than a bully And I happen to know that poor Lord Sinclair and David had a massive argument in the living
textByHash.f0577dd0ccdbcbb17217ce2dc9a51570594cd26b: What are you doing here? I thought the house was locked up tight.
textByHash.f1d3cae3a0fabd82605a011d05722c13727c8c6b: There were various chores around the house that required poison.
textByHash.f29151226985bfd3896445fd886a4a04e9771290: You know, now you come to mention it I don't believe I did. I suppose that is Proof enough that it could not have been an intruder who is responsible.
